active_question: When a rope model becomes non-verbal, who does Anatomie Studio say has the immediate responsibility to check in?
edited_answer: Safety remains a joint responsibility, but the rigger has the immediate responsibility to check in.

support_evidence: Committing to take joint responsibility is a great way to relieve some of the pressure. However, it’s important to note that models can go non-verbal in rope, or simply sink into a space in which communication means they lose their headspace. In this case the immediate responsibility will fall to the rigger to check in.
